title: Four Blocks Course — Emotional Formulas
subtitle: Canonical Formulas for Anger, Anxiety, Depression, and Guilt
source: Dr. Vincent E. Parr — My Four Blocks Course
purpose: Define the four canonical emotional formulas that identify the thought patterns and beliefs driving emotional disturbance, enabling individuals to dispute and replace them with rational, healthier beliefs.

chunks.title: Formula for Anger

components.label: Egocentric Thinking
components.definition: Placing oneself at the center of the universe and believing that reality should conform to one's preferences.
components.emotional_role: Creates the foundation of anger by generating a rigid expectation that the world must align with personal desires.

components.label: Should
components.definition: The belief that something should or must be different than it is, leading to frustration and anger.
components.emotional_role: Converts preference into rigid demand, locking anger in place when reality fails to comply.

chunks.intervention_target: Soften the Should (S) from rigid demand into strong preference while keeping values intact.

chunks.title: Formula for Anxiety
chunks.expanded: Anxiety = What If? + Awfulizing + I Can't Stand It

components.label: What If
components.definition: Projecting the mind into a future that does not exist and imagining negative possibilities.
components.emotional_role: Initiates anxiety by pulling attention away from the present and into fearful future scenarios.

components.label: Awfulizing
components.definition: Exaggerating the unpleasantness of a situation to an extreme degree.
components.emotional_role: Amplifies the imagined future threat beyond realistic proportion, intensifying anxiety.

components.label: I Can't Stand It
components.definition: Believing that one cannot tolerate or survive the imagined negative outcome.
components.emotional_role: Converts difficulty into perceived impossibility, locking anxiety in place by removing any sense of coping capacity.

chunks.intervention_target: Soften each component separately: normalize What If possibilities, distinguish difficult from catastrophic, reinforce distress tolerance.

chunks.title: Formula for Depression

components.label: Hopelessness
components.definition: A pessimistic outlook toward the past, present, and future.
components.emotional_role: Creates the belief that nothing has worked, nothing works now, and nothing will ever work, eliminating motivation and forward movement.

components.label: Helplessness
components.definition: Feeling powerless to change one's situation, often accompanied by self-pity, other-pity, and self-rejection.
components.emotional_role: Removes perceived agency, reinforcing the belief that change is impossible regardless of effort.

components.label: Need
components.definition: A false belief that one requires something such as love, success, comfort, or certainty to be happy, leading to feelings of inadequacy and despair.
components.emotional_role: Converts wants into rigid requirements, creating despair when those perceived needs go unmet. True needs are limited to food, water, and shelter; everything else is a want.

chunks.intervention_target: Convert the rigid Need (N) into a strong preference or wish, which softens Hopelessness and Helplessness and allows depression to lift.

chunks.title: Formula for Guilt

components.label: Wrongness
components.definition: The belief that one has done something wrong or failed to meet a standard, often accompanied by a Should.
components.emotional_role: Creates the foundation of guilt by judging a past action or omission as a moral failure.

components.label: Worthlessness
components.definition: Rating oneself as inferior or bad because of perceived mistakes or failures.
components.emotional_role: Fuses the wrongness judgment into a global identity condemnation, converting honest regret into crushing self-punishment.

chunks.intervention_target: Separate W1 from W2. Wrongness (W1) can be honestly acknowledged while Worthlessness (W2) is disputed, preserving accountability without self-condemnation.

chunks.title: Emotional Formulas — Unified Reference
chunks.content: The Four Blocks system identifies four canonical emotional formulas that map the precise thought structures driving each emotional disturbance. Anger: A = ET + S (Egocentric Thinking + Should). Anxiety: AX = WI + AW + ICSI (What If + Awfulizing + I Can't Stand It). Depression: D = H1 + H2 + N (Hopelessness + Helplessness + Need). Guilt: G = W1 + W2 (Wrongness + Worthlessness). Each formula identifies the specific irrational beliefs fueling the emotion, enabling targeted disputation and replacement with rational, healthier beliefs.
